name: frozen_arm_base_lift_place
discovered: 2026-07-02, AlohaMini Pro NAV/MANIP pick-and-place (final placement fix)

symptoms: the held object is lost somewhere between a successful carry and the release
closed-loop corrections report converged IK but the object does not follow the TCP
placement error 70-190 mm that no release-height / approach-branch tweak fixes
trace_evidence: object tracking shows held=False right after any ARM reconfiguration block (carry config -> pre-place config), even at descend speed and with branch-continuous IK seeding
lowering the LIFT with the object held pressed the hanging object into the tabletop and popped it out (lift 0.16->0 = 16 cm blind drop vs ~9 cm of clearance)
raising gripper force 15->40 N ejected the object during the CLOSE instead (watermelon-seed effect)

when_to_apply: Mobile manipulator with a weak/flat-pad gripper carrying an object to a placement: any joint-space arm reconfiguration while holding tends to sling/twist the object out; the base and lift are far gentler DOFs.
repair_strategy: Do not move the ARM at all between grasp and release. Keep the arm frozen in the carry configuration; do the horizontal fine-alignment with the BASE (closed-loop: re-measure the object, drive base by (target - object), 1-3 rounds), then descend with the LIFT ONLY by the measured amount (object_z - (surface + clearance)), settle, release, and retreat by raising the lift. The mobile platform is the precision mechanism; the arm only manipulates when the gripper is empty.
code_sketch: # after NAV-2 (carry), arm stays at desc_q forever
for _ in range(3):   # closed-loop base alignment
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro nav pick-and-place with REAL table+floor collision: placed=True, 10 mm error (from 78-190 mm with arm-motion variants)
